body:
## Severity: MEDIUM

## Game Affected
All games (every NIF carrying NiNode hits the read_block_ref_list site twice per node).

## Description

Source: AUDIT_NIF_2026-04-28.md (NIF-D3-09 + NIF-D3-10).

`NifStream::read_block_ref_list` reads a `u32` count off the stream and pre-allocates the destination vector with raw `Vec::with_capacity(count as usize)` — bypassing the `allocate_vec` budget guard that every other size-prefixed reader uses (cap = 256 MB single allocation, count ≤ remaining stream bytes). The same pattern recurs at 6 additional sites that use `Vec::reserve` / `Vec::with_capacity` on file-driven `u32` lengths.

### Primary site (D3-09)

```rust
// crates/nif/src/stream.rs:439-446 — UNGUARDED
pub fn read_block_ref_list(&mut self) -> io::Result<Vec<BlockRef>> {
    let count = self.read_u32_le()? as usize;
    let mut refs = Vec::with_capacity(count);   // ← bypasses allocate_vec
    for _ in 0..count {
        refs.push(self.read_block_ref()?);
    }
    Ok(refs)
}
```

8 callers depend on this helper:
- `blocks/base.rs:44, 101` — `NiObjectNETData.extra_data_refs`, `NiAVObjectData.properties`
- `blocks/node.rs:102, 109` — `NiNode.children`, `NiNode.effects`
- `blocks/node.rs:313, 314` — `BsTreeNode.bones_1`, `bones_2`
- `blocks/particle.rs:906, 910` — particle subtree refs

`NiNode::parse` runs the helper twice per node; a 1000-node skeleton calls it 2,000 times.

### Sibling sites (D3-10) — six `.reserve()` / `with_capacity()` calls on file-driven counts

- `crates/nif/src/blocks/shader.rs:443, 447` — `parse_skyrim_shader_base` (BSSkyShaderProperty / BSWaterShaderProperty)
- `crates/nif/src/blocks/shader.rs:902, 906` — `BSLightingShaderProperty` BSVER ≥ 132 branch (Skyrim SE / FO4 — hottest call site)
- `crates/nif/src/blocks/shader.rs:1502, 1506` — `BSEffectShaderProperty` BSVER ≥ 132 branch
- `crates/nif/src/blocks/interpolator.rs:304` — `NiTransformData` rotation_keys
- `crates/nif/src/blocks/bs_geometry.rs:465` — `BSGeometryMeshData` per-LOD triangle Vec

Plain `Vec::reserve` aborts on capacity overflow (no `try_reserve` here).

## Impact

Untrusted-NIF parsing (mod loader, asset hot-reload watcher, network DLC) can OOM-panic the process from any block carrying a corrupt block-ref-list count. The OOM is in `Vec::with_capacity` **before** any read happens, so block-size recovery cannot help. A `count = 0xFFFFFFFF` requests ~16 GB of capacity for `Vec<BlockRef>` before the first `read_i32_le` fails.

Bounded today by inner-read EOF on the trusted-corpus path, but the policy invariant ("every file-driven u32 length goes through `allocate_vec` / `check_alloc`") is what closed #388 and #113 for every other bulk reader; this is the missed call site.

## Suggested Fix

Route the count through `allocate_vec` for the primary site:

```rust
pub fn read_block_ref_list(&mut self) -> io::Result<Vec<BlockRef>> {
    let count = self.read_u32_le()?;
    let mut refs = self.allocate_vec(count)?;
    for _ in 0..count {
        refs.push(self.read_block_ref()?);
    }
    Ok(refs)
}
```

Each `BlockRef` consumes 4 on-disk bytes (i32 length + value), so the existing `count > remaining` budget check is conservative but correct. No call-site changes; signature unchanged.

For the six sibling sites, replace `Vec::with_capacity(count as usize)` / `vec.reserve(count as usize)` with `stream.allocate_vec(count)?`. Mechanical cleanup; counts are already `u32`.

## Related

- Audit: docs/audits/AUDIT_NIF_2026-04-28.md (NIF-D3-09 + NIF-D3-10)
- Prior: #388, #113 (this is the missed call site from those policy sweeps)

## Completeness Checks

- [ ] **UNSAFE**: N/A — no unsafe blocks affected
- [ ] **SIBLING**: Audit the rest of the parser via `grep -nE "(\.reserve\(|with_capacity\()" crates/nif/src/` to confirm no other file-driven counts remain
- [ ] **DROP**: N/A
- [ ] **LOCK_ORDER**: N/A
- [ ] **FFI**: N/A
- [ ] **TESTS**: Add a malformed-NIF fixture with `count = 0xFFFFFFFF` for `NiObjectNETData.extra_data_refs` and assert `Err(InvalidData)` rather than abort
title: NIF-D3-09: read_block_ref_list + 6 reserve sites bypass allocate_vec — DoS via corrupt count
